mnemonics: ACID Rain Keeps Data Dry: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability - the properties of a transaction.
DBA Views Schema: Data (DML), Definition (DDL), Control (DCL) - the three types of database languages.
keyPoints: A DBMS is software that manages databases, providing an environment for efficient data storage, retrieval, and manipulation.
The three-schema architecture (Internal, Conceptual, External) provides data independence, separating user applications from the physical database.
Data models (e.g., Relational, Hierarchical) provide a conceptual framework for describing the structure of a database.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Database (data store) with the DBMS (the management software).
Assuming logical data independence (changing the conceptual schema) is as easy as physical data independence (changing the internal schema).
